items.final_wording: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

items.rationale: Supports contact-lens history and conjunctivitis diagnostic pitfalls for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed eye red flags for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative
source.population: Contact-lens wearers with suspected contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis features and safe lens-care documentation; UAE escalation pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical summary — presentation and differential safety

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

items.rationale: Supports contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis presentation for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)
source.population: People with suspected bacterial or fungal microbial keratitis.
source.setting: Primary, first-contact, and specialist eye care.
source.exclusions: Exact for sight-threatening keratitis features and emergency referral context; UAE pathways and treatment require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Patient summary — sight-threatening emergency and referral

source.evidence_paraphrase: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

items.rationale: Supports sight-threatening keratitis emergency context for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

added_items.rationale: Supports contact-lens history and conjunctivitis diagnostic pitfalls for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

added_items.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed eye red flags for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative
source.population: Contact-lens wearers with suspected contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis.
source.exclusions: Exact for contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis features and safe lens-care documentation; UAE escalation pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical summary — presentation and differential safety

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

added_items.rationale: Supports contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis presentation for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.

added_items.final_wording: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.
